nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef and intertidal zones with moderate to high water movement

decorPreferences: open placement or loose attachment to rockwork

avoidWith: aggressive herbivorous fish that may graze heavily on macroalgae

imageCandidates.notes: Product photo from the Mosaic Macros listing for Bluish-Purple Ochtodes. Only known hobby source for this specialty species.

summary: A specialty ornamental red macroalga (Rhodophyta) with cylindrical branching thallus, displaying an iridescent bluish-purple hue under full-spectrum lighting. Available primarily through Mosaic Macros.
careNotes:
Ochtodes sp. is a red alga (Rhodophyta) in the family Rhizophyllidaceae, order Gigartinales, with a cylindrical, dichotomously branching thallus. The bluish-purple iridescence is a structural coloration effect — specimens tend to appear grey or muted upon arrival and generally color up over several days once placed under adequate full-spectrum lighting. This coloration shift is normal and does not indicate a health problem.

As a Mosaic Macros specialty listing, this is the sole known hobby source at time of research; care parameters below are extrapolated from family-level and genus-level literature and corroborated by typical ornamental red macroalgae husbandry. Generally advised to maintain standard reef salinity (SG 1.023–1.026), temperature 23–28°C, and pH 8.1–8.4. Medium lighting (moderate full-spectrum) is generally recommended for this class of red ornamental macroalgae; excessive PAR has been reported to cause bleaching or browning in related species. Moderate to high flow helps deliver nutrients and prevents debris accumulation on the thallus. No supplemental fertilization is typically required in an established reef system, though trace dosing of iron and iodine may benefit growth.

Ochtodes spp. have been documented producing halogenated monoterpenes (myrcene and related compounds) with mild antimicrobial properties; no allelopathic effects on corals have been reported at typical display densities, and the species is generally considered reef-safe. Propagation in the aquarium is most reliably achieved by fragmentation — detached pieces tend to form new growth points when secured to rockwork or rubble.

Note: the Mosaic Macros product URL slug references 'copy-blue-hypnea-macroalgae-hypnea-pannosa', but the listing is explicitly for Ochtodes sp. (Bluish-Purple Ochtodes), a distinct genus from Hypnea. Ochtodes has noticeably thicker, more cylindrical branches and a deeper purple hue compared to Hypnea pannosa. The scientific name should not be confused with the URL artifact. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: Aquarium propagation is typically by fragmentation: detaching a portion of the thallus and anchoring it to rockwork or rubble where it can reattach and grow. Sexual reproduction via alternation of gametophyte and sporophyte generations occurs in wild populations but has not been documented as a practical propagation route in the hobby.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Only known hobby trade source for this specialty species. Confirms scientific name Ochtodes sp., iridescent bluish-purple coloration, cylindrical branching form, and grey-on-arrival coloration behavior. No numeric water parameters provided by source.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn — Blue Hypnea (Hypnea pannosa) care page
additional.notes: Used as secondary reference for Gigartinales red macroalgae water parameters (temperature 73–83°F / 23–28°C, SG 1.023–1.025, pH 7.8–8.4). Ochtodes and Hypnea both belong to order Gigartinales, supporting parameter extrapolation. Confirmed medium lighting and fragmentation propagation for this group.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
